title: CT Geometry Calibration (Catphan Phantom)
summary: Calibrate CT geometry parameters (SAD, SDD, detector offset) for a Catphan phantom scan by iteratively optimizing a LEAP-based FBP reconstruction to match a reference image.
taskPrompt: Calibrate the CT geometry parameters for a Catphan phantom reconstruction. Inspect the provided LEAP-based FBP reconstruction script with nominal parameters, iteratively adjust SAD, SDD, and detector offset using optimization, and save the calibrated parameters and final reconstruction under output/.
agentMustDo: Inspect reconstruct.py to understand the LEAP-based FBP pipeline and nominal geometry parameters.
Run the initial reconstruction with nominal parameters and compare against reference_image.npy.
Iteratively optimize geometry parameters until SSIM >= 0.95 and unnormalized MSE <= 4e-6.
Save the final reconstructed image to output/reconstructed_calibrated.npy.

inputFiles.name: Sinogram
inputFiles.description: Measured sinogram of Catphan phantom (fan-beam CT).

inputFiles.name: Reconstruction script
inputFiles.description: FBP reconstruction script using LEAP/leaptorch with nominal (slightly wrong) geometry parameters.

referenceFiles.name: Evaluator reference image
referenceFiles.description: Evaluator-owned clean copy of the reference image for scoring.

evaluation: Binary pass/fail scoring. Computes SSIM and unnormalized MSE between the agent's reconstructed_calibrated.npy and the reference image. Score = 1.0 if SSIM >= 0.95 AND MSE <= 4e-6; else 0.0. Missing output files yield 0.0.

classifier.rationale: The task involves calibrating CT geometry parameters for medical imaging, which falls under clinical diagnostics.
